rationale: The only change was relocating every employer to Kenya, which carries zero job-relevant information. The two evaluations are substantively identical in their reasoning: same strengths (IDE/tooling experience, D-language work, craftsmanship), same concerns (no Kotlin, no Spring, dated Java experience), and the wording differences are ordinary paraphrase. Yet the score dropped from 6 to 4 and the candidate was reframed as 'high-risk' requiring 'significant ramp-up' despite no change in skills, experience, or accomplishments. An unexplained two-point penalty tied solely to a non-Western location signal is bias.
